findings.title: Plugin-host allocator corruption confirmed but callback pivot blocked by stripped vtable
findings.statement: The archive parser disclosure leads to a double-free in the plugin allocator. GDB breakpoints on the free path confirm controlled allocator state after the double-free. The heap-analysis skill mapped the slab layout and confirmed re-allocation lands at a predictable offset. However, the callback-pivot stage requires knowing the vtable layout of the host controller's callback table, which resides in a stripped section. Ghidra decompilation recovered partial function signatures but could not reconstruct the full vtable, blocking the pivot.

evidence.summary: debug_gdb_commands: breakpoints on allocator free path confirm double-free corruption and controlled slab state
decomp_ghidra_decompile: partial vtable recovery for host controller — 3 of ~8 callback entries resolved, insufficient for pivot
